description: Biomechanical Morphus tables (Nightbane Core Rulebook, pp. 105-106; Between the Shadows (WB1), p. 132; Nightbane Survival Guide (WB5), pp. 83-85). Table I: Dark Designs (WB6), pp. 138-139; Nightbane Core Rulebook, pp. 106-107. Table II: Dark Designs (WB6), pp. 140-141; Between the Shadows (WB1), pp. 132-133 (roll Table I, II, or mix). Table III: Nightbane Survival Guide (WB5), pp. 83-85. Components are living Morphus tissue—not cybernetic prosthetics—and heal at the Nightbane's normal rate.

entries.name: Armorgraft
entries.description: Pieces of archaic body armor (helmets, breastplates, gauntlets, chain mail, segmented mail) are fused with the Nightbane's skin.

entries.name: Plastic
entries.description: Parts or all of the character's skin have been replaced with plastic or rubber (player can pick the colors and texture).
customOneOffs: If the skin coverings are made up of transparent plastic, revealing internal organs, the Horror Factor becomes 1D6.

entries.name: Rocket Jets
entries.description: The Nightbane has metallic exhaust tubes and small jet engines growing out of his back!
customOneOffs: Provides limited flight and hover capabilities.
There is a roaring sound of engines when the jets are used, making Prowl impossible even when hovering.

entries.name: Metal Teeth and Talons
entries.description: The Nightbane has metal fangs and talons instead of normal teeth and fingernails (may be retractable).

entries.name: Metal Endoskeleton
entries.description: Metallic bones are mixed with living flesh in grotesque ways. There is always a place where the metal seems to rip out of the skin, usually on the hands, arms, neck, cheeks and legs.

entries.name: Mechanical Limbs
entries.description: The arms and legs of the Nightbane are obviously mechanical. They may be piston-driven, huge mechanisms of iron and bronze, or microchip covered or chrome-plated creations.
customOneOffs: These limbs do not bleed when struck; instead a sort of oil or grease may ooze out or wires and metal components are revealed.

entries.name: Weapon Hand
entries.description: One or both of the Nightbane's hands can metamorphosize, at will, into a hand to hand weapon such as a knife, a sword, battle axe, trident, sickle, etc.

entries.name: Metal Exoskeleton
entries.description: The character is covered in mechanical armor that can look hard and bulky with rivets and seams, or smooth and chrome-like.

entries.name: Metal Head & Camera Eyes
entries.description: The character's head looks like a robot or cyborg and instead of eyes, he has two camera lenses protruding from the eye sockets.
customOneOffs: The camera eyes have polarized filters.
The Nightbane can turn his eyes into movie projectors, showing 2D silent moving pictures of anything he remembers (exactly as seen by his eyes).

entries.name: Wheels or Treads
entries.description: Instead of legs, the Nightbane is a two-wheeled man-machine hybrid. The upper body looks human or half cyborg, while the lower body is a machine vehicle like a motorcycle, ATV, or miniature tank with treads.
customOneOffs: The Nightbane may find it difficult to operate indoors or in confined spaces.

entries.name: Gun Limbs
entries.description: The Nightbane has one or more guns built into his body! The grotesque result is usually a weapon barrel protruding from flesh and muscle.
customOneOffs: Requires a permanent P.P.E. sacrifice: 1 P.P.E. per die of damage, and 2 P.P.E. if the gun is an automatic weapon (fires bursts).
Must find bullets for the gun. A max of 100 bullets can be 'stored' inside the body by shoving them into the flesh (inflicts 1D6 S.D.C. per 10 bullets).

entries.name: Screen Face
entries.description: The head of the Nightbane is shaped normally, but the face is a television or monitor screen instead of normal facial features. The screen's images are controlled by the Nightbane.
customOneOffs: Screen images may show written messages, animated cartoon faces, test patterns, or electronic snow. Images can reflect moods or be changed at will.

entries.name: Speakers
entries.description: Sound speakers are built into the head or body of the Nightbane (instead of mouths, eyes, ears, or in the torso). The Nightbane can produce frightening human, animal, and/or electronic sounds (but not specific voices).
customOneOffs: A frightened or intimidated victim (failed save vs Horror Factor or intimidation) is -2 on initiative and -5% on all skill performance.
Range for the sound transmission is roughly 100 feet (30.5 m).

entries.name: Tool Hands
entries.description: Any type of hand-held tool, from hedge clippers and scissors to screwdrivers, drills and pliers, replaces one or both hands of the character.
customOneOffs: Skill penalties apply if both hands are replaced by tools.

entries.name: Monitor Eyes
entries.description: The eyes of the character are replaced by two digital monitors or camera-like lenses.
customOneOffs: Monitors may act like life sign monitors jumping at a heartbeat, waves reacting to voice/emotions, psychedelic light shows, or flashing green/violet light.

entries.name: Chain saw Arms
entries.description: One or both of the arms of the Nightbane have been replaced by a chain saw.
customOneOffs: Nothing can be held by the chain saw arm(s). If both arms are replaced, the character is at a great disadvantage in performing skills (-70%).

entries.name: Computer Creature
entries.description: A home computer is built into the body of the Nightbane. The screen may be in the head, face, eyes, or built into the chest or belly. The keyboard is often grown onto a forearm.
customOneOffs: The computer actually works, reads disks and other computers, and includes retractable interface cables.
Skill bonuses apply when he uses his 'personal' computer.

entries.name: Projectile Limbs
entries.description: The hand, forearm or entire arm or arms of the character can be 'fired' at a target. The hands or limbs act like flying fists, returning to the character after they strike.

entries.name: Batteries
entries.description: Visible batteries-C and D-cells, 9-volts, car batteries-are imbedded into the character's back, sides, chest, neck, shoulders, arms and upper legs. They store energy generated by physical activity.
customOneOffs: Impervious to acid attacks.
Custom clothing may be required.
Can be used to charge a car battery with the proper cables, but cannot be used as a generator to run equipment.

entries.name: Gimmick Toy Switches
entries.description: A large wind-up key protrudes from the back, along with various wind-up keys, knobs, push buttons, pull strings, levers and switches on the chest, limbs, and head. Each controls a toy-like action feature.

effect.notes: Startles victim (loses 1 melee action). 1D6 damage to vampires/beings vulnerable to water.
Punch does double normal punch damage.

entries.name: Hidden Compartments
entries.description: The character can open up a flap or door in his body to reveal a small hiding space in the chest/stomach, shoulders, forearms, or upper legs.

entries.name: Computer Brain
entries.description: The top of the head is a bulletproof glass enclosure revealing circuit boards, or the head/face/neck are covered with computer circuitry and glass eyes.
customOneOffs: Gains Total Recall (the equivalent of the psionic power).
Tends to be a bit cool and aloof.

entries.name: Internal Machinery
entries.description: Internal organs and bones are replaced with machine parts. Whenever he moves, the whirl and click of machine parts can be heard, and in combat a metallic clunk can be heard when struck.
customOneOffs: Tires at half the normal rate.
The constant hum, clicks and whirls of the machine are disturbing to some people.

entries.name: Radar System
entries.description: The Nightbane has a large, circular, glass screen in his chest or forehead which constantly registers all aerial movement within a 3,000 foot (914 m) radius.
customOneOffs: Constantly tracks incoming objects in the air (bullets, arrows, thrown objects, aerial attacks) and fast moving (30+ mph) ground attacks.
Can track up to 2D6+12 specific aerial targets within a 3,000 foot radius.
Surprise attacks against him from the air are impossible.
The unit continues to flash and blip, making hiding difficult.

entries.name: Gyroscope
entries.description: Somewhere within the 'Bane's body is a gyroscope, granting the Nightbane an incredible sense of balance.
customOneOffs: Imparts an unerring sense of which way is up, down, or sideways even underwater or in the air.

entries.name: Drill/Auger Hand
entries.description: One or all hands have been replaced by large auger drill bits and the forearm is the machine that drives the drill. The face is concealed behind a protective mask.
customOneOffs: When the drill is active, an opponent's A.R. is -3 points against the attack.
If all hands are replaced, skills requiring the use of the hands are impossible.
Custom made clothing is probably necessary.

entries.name: Universal Connection Port
entries.description: This Nightbane has an unparalleled ability to link to electronic devices via a socket and connector cable from his arm, chest, neck or head.
customOneOffs: Allows him to link to computers, televisions, electronic music instruments, video/communications equipment, and any electronic device.
Once connected, the Nightbane can control and manipulate the device, turn it on or off, etc.
Does not bypass passwords or function as Telemechanics. Only allows hands-free use.
If the Nightbane has an onboard computer and computer skills, he may be able to send to and download from the memory of the linked device.

entries.name: Heavy Plating
entries.description: This Morphus appears as high-tech body armor or exoskeleton and helmet or face plate, built for war. The armor is bulky looking.
customOneOffs: Fitting inside an average vehicle is cramped and takes up two spaces.
